text: New membrane materials require innovative, tailored, efficient and chemically stable separation processes. The specific separation of rare earth elements (REEs) is a newly emerging area of membrane technology and resources demand. The optimization of the 7 chemical separation processes including REEs extraction has been identified as a priority area with high global impact. Membrane technology, as a simple, sustainable “green” technology has been successfully demonstrated in metal separation and wastewater treatment processes. However, a clear overview of the literature concerning REEs extraction and membrane processes is not yet available. There is an urgent need to advance liquid membranes to solid (non-liquid) membranes, simply because liquid membranes are plagued with poor stability, heterogeneous extraction performance etc. This article provides an overview of the non-liquid membranes for REEs separation.
